title: National Bird Day Takes Flight!
story: Every January 5, bird lovers across America celebrate National Bird Day by watching, learning about, and protecting our feathered friends! People grab their binoculars and head to parks, backyards, and nature centers to spot as many different birds as possible. Many families visit bird sanctuaries or zoos to see exotic species up close. Kids often make homemade bird feeders using pinecones, peanut butter, and seeds to hang outside their windows. Social media fills with amazing bird photos and fun facts about everything from tiny hummingbirds to massive eagles. It's the perfect day to appreciate these incredible flying creatures!
activity_question: What do people commonly use to make homemade bird feeders on National Bird Day?
bonus_fact: Some birds can see ultraviolet colors that humans can't even imagine!

title: Whipped Cream Dreams Come True Day
story: Every January 5, people across America celebrate National Whipped Cream Day by adding fluffy white clouds of deliciousness to everything they can think of! Coffee shops see lines of customers ordering extra whipped cream on their hot chocolate and lattes. Families make special desserts like strawberries and cream, ice cream sundaes, and whipped cream-topped pies. Some brave souls even eat whipped cream straight from the can! Social media fills with photos of towering whipped cream creations. Whether it's homemade with a whisk or store-bought from a spray can, this sweet holiday proves that everything tastes better with a dollop of fluffy goodness on top.
activity_question: What do coffee shops notice on National Whipped Cream Day?
activity_choices: Lines of customers ordering extra whipped cream
People bringing their own whipped cream
activity_answer: Lines of customers ordering extra whipped cream
follow_up_question: What's the most creative thing you could add whipped cream to?

title: Twelfth Night's Magical End to Christmas
story: Every January 5, people celebrate Twelfth Night, the magical final evening of the Christmas season! This special night marks twelve days after Christmas, when families traditionally take down their Christmas trees and decorations. In many countries, people gather for festive parties with special cakes, singing, and storytelling. Some families hide a bean or coin inside a special cake, and whoever finds it becomes "king" or "queen" for the night! It's like one last Christmas party before winter gets serious.
activity_question: What do many families traditionally hide inside a special Twelfth Night cake?
bonus_fact: Shakespeare wrote a famous play called "Twelfth Night" about this holiday!

title: Scripts and Stories Day!
story: Every January 5, writers who create movie and TV scripts get their own special day! National Screenwriters Day celebrates the creative minds behind your favorite films and shows. People share their favorite movie quotes online, post about screenwriters who inspire them, and some even try writing their own short scripts. Film schools and writing groups often host special events or workshops on this day to honor these storytellers who bring characters to life on screen.
activity_question: What do people often share online on National Screenwriters Day?
follow_up_question: What movie or TV show would you want to write a script for?

title: The Night Before the Big Reveal
story: Tonight is Epiphany Eve, the magical night before January 5 when many families around the world prepare for one of the most exciting gift-giving days of the year! Kids in Spain, Mexico, and other countries go to bed extra early because tomorrow, the Three Wise Men will arrive with presents. Families leave out shoes by their doors, along with grass and water for the camels. Some children even write letters to the kings, asking for special gifts and promising to be good all year long.
activity_question: What do children leave out for the camels on Epiphany Eve?
follow_up_question: What would you ask the Three Wise Men for if they visited your house?
